comment.body:
Has anyone read about China’s dominance in auto manufacturing? They are the largest auto exporter in the world now and absolutely dominating their sphere of influence. What is Mercedes or BMW gonna do? Sell a $60K car to a family in Africa or Vietnam or India?

They better start producing more economical makes/models if they want to compete.

And is China gonna let them access their middle/upper class? Highly doubt it.

comment.body:
Alma did say algos are in mean reversion mode, even though it is becoming less and less pronounced. Seems though that we should go higher and it seems highly probably given sentiment… although what’s weird is that there is not a lot of hedging to the downside. So as Alma has alluded to, the market is structurally set up to continue down. Moreover, even though she does say vol should come down, if you look at 22’, VIX never pierced below $18.50 outside of January. And it raged up to $34ish for its highs.

We are coming off a $16.50 low, and during 22’, we’ve seen those wild spikes where it didn’t need to consolidate at these levels. So we are really in a coin toss environment here. Although it still does seem like further upside is likely.
